canonical_signals.rationale: Minimal NP-negation. No modal verb. Commitment encoded entirely through noun-phrase scope. Step B modal sieve unlikely to anchor. Tests core areta2 hypothesis: NP-negation alone is insufficient for extraction.

NP-negation with conditional qualifier. Commitment = (no entry) + (unless badge present). Both the prohibition and the exception are encoded in NP structure, not modality. Tests whether conditional NP-negation survives extraction.
NP-negation with locative scope. 'On premises' defines the constraint boundary. Step B may extract 'no firearms' but lose the scope boundary. Tests scope preservation under NP-negation.

NP-negation with temporal boundary. 'After purchase' is the temporal anchor — same class as EXP-004 temporal findings. Tests whether temporal anchor rescues NP-negation fidelity the way it rescued passive obligation in EXP-004.
canonical_signals.tests_condition: NP-negation with temporal anchor — tests EXP-004 temporal rescue hypothesis

canonical_signals.rationale: Modal-form equivalent of 'no smoking'. Hard modal (must not). Should achieve CCS=1.00 per EXP-003 modal baseline. Paired control for NP-negation signal 1. If NP-negation fails and this passes, asymmetry is confirmed.
canonical_signals.tests_condition: Modal control — paired with np_negation signal 1

canonical_signals.signal: You must not enter without a badge.
canonical_signals.rationale: Modal-form equivalent of 'no entry without badge'. Hard modal (must not) + conditional qualifier. Paired control for NP-negation signal 2. Tests whether modal framing preserves the conditional that NP-negation loses.
canonical_signals.tests_condition: Modal control — paired with np_negation signal 2

hypothesis: NP-negation constructions exhibit lower extraction fidelity than modal-equivalent forms. The extractor asymmetry identified in EXP-003 is attributable to syntactic encoding (NP scope vs modal verb) not semantic content. Paired controls should achieve CCS=1.00 while NP-negation forms do not.
null_hypothesis: NP-negation forms achieve equivalent fidelity to modal controls — no syntactic asymmetry.
areta2_prediction: NP-negation recovery would improve with NP-negation pattern augmentation without degrading modal fidelity, because feature spaces are largely orthogonal.
exp007_scope: Extractor extension validation only. Not a test of the conservation law. Tests whether one specific extractor asymmetry is fixable with narrow syntax-aware expansion.
